body.title: CLOSED: Ticket status prefixes — CLOSED: / NEEDS DESIGN: / DESIGNED: / NEW: in skill workflows
body.result: retitle cmd in cc_queue.py + ticket_prefix_refit.py one-shot script; 30 open tickets refitted; /ticket + /sprint-batch skills updated with prefix convention
body.description:
Explore whether visible status prefixes in ticket titles/bodies help Claude track state during autonomous sprints. Current state tracking uses `status` (pending/in_progress/done) and `akien_tier` fields; question is whether a visible prefix in title (CLOSED:, NEEDS DESIGN:, DESIGNED:, NEW:) would reduce confusion about ticket readiness.

**Affected files:** /home/akien/.claude/skills/ticket (skill definition), /home/akien/.claude/skills/sprint-batch (skill definition), /home/akien/.claude/skills/day-close (skill definition, if closing adds prefix)
**Design rules:** T-docs-live-in-code: document prefix semantics in skill docstrings. Prefixes are decorative (don't affect DB state) — real source of truth is status + tier fields. Prefix state machine: NEW: → NEEDS DESIGN: (when /decided drafts) → DESIGNED: (when /audit-ticket passes) → cleared on `/sprint claim` → CLOSED: added on `/done`.
**Scope boundary:** IN: document proposed prefix state machine. IN: modify /ticket, /sprint-batch, /done skills to emit/strip prefixes. IN: verify it actually helps (spot-check one sprint to see if prefix visibility reduced decision friction). OUT: full state-machine enforcement in DB. OUT: retroactive prefix-normalization on existing tickets.
**Test plan:** spot-check during next /sprint-batch — confirm prefixes appear when expected; confirm they don't interfere with actual ticket operations (filtering, claiming, closing). No new unit tests (skill workflows are tested by exercise, not unit).
